content:
✨ reverse-SynthID | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Detecting and removing invisible AI watermarks embedded in images by Google's SynthID system.
✨ Key Features: • Reverse-engineering SynthID watermark • Spectral analysis for detection and removal • Resolution-dependent carrier frequency discovery • 90% accurate watermark detector • Multi-resolution spectral bypass (V3)
📖 Summary: This repository reverse-engineers Google's SynthID AI watermarking system using signal processing and spectral analysis. It discovers the watermark's structure, builds a detector with 90% accuracy, and develops a multi-resolution spectral bypass to surgically remove the watermark.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ developer-icons | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide developers and designers with a readily available and easily integrable set of technology logos for use in websites, applications, and design projects.
✨ Key Features: • Collection of well-optimized SVG tech logos • Customizable and scalable • Available as an npm package • Free and open-source • Compatible with TypeScript, React, and Next.js
📖 Summary: This repository provides a collection of well-optimized SVG logos for various technologies, designed for developers and designers. The icons are customizable, scalable, and available as an npm package, making them easy to integrate into projects.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 livestore | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To serve as a client-centric data layer for applications, enabling offline-first capabilities and real-time data synchronization.
✨ Key Features: • Reactive query layer with full SQLite support • Adapters for web, mobile, server/edge, desktop • Flexible data modeling and schema management • True offline-first workflows • Custom merge conflict resolution
📖 Summary: LiveStore is a next-generation state management framework that provides a powerful, client-centric data foundation for applications. It leverages reactive SQLite and a built-in sync engine to enable true offline-first workflows with custom merge conflict resolution and adapters for various platforms.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 Baileys | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Developing custom applications and bots that integrate with WhatsApp.
✨ Key Features: • WebSockets-based API for WhatsApp Web • TypeScript library • Programmatic interaction with WhatsApp features • Message sending and receiving • Contact management
📖 Summary: Baileys is a TypeScript library for interacting with the WhatsApp Web API using WebSockets. It allows developers to build applications that can send and receive messages, manage contacts, and interact with WhatsApp features programmatically.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 nowinandroid | Kotlin
🎯 Primary Use Case: To serve as a reference for Android development best practices using Kotlin and Jetpack Compose, and to keep developers informed about the latest Android news and content.
✨ Key Features: • Built entirely with Kotlin and Jetpack Compose • Follows Android design and development best practices • Provides regular news updates about Android development • Allows users to browse content like videos and articles • Enables users to follow topics and receive notifications
📖 Summary: Now in Android is a fully functional Android application built with Kotlin and Jetpack Compose, demonstrating best practices in Android development. It serves as a reference for developers and provides up-to-date news and content about the Android ecosystem.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
